description.value: Native graph database for storing and querying highly connected data using nodes and relationships
description.comment: ACID-compliant native graph DBMS, the most popular graph database per DB-Engines.

license.comment: Community Edition is GPLv3; Enterprise Edition is closed-source under a commercial license.

latest-version.comment: Calendar-versioned release 2026.05.0 (released 28 May 2026); LTS branch 5.26.x also maintained. Checked 2026-06-09.

query-language.comment: Cypher query language, via Bolt protocol or transactional HTTP endpoint.

schema-model.comment: Schema-optional: nodes/relationships are flexible, but optional constraints and indexes can be defined.

horizontal-scaling.comment: Built-in sharding / federated queries (Enterprise; Infinigraph architecture) distribute data across servers.

replication.comment: Leader-based clustering (Raft); primary-secondary replication. Enterprise Edition only.

clustering.comment: Built-in multi-server clustering in Enterprise Edition; Community Edition is single-instance.

cloud-offering.comment: Neo4j AuraDB is the official fully-managed cloud service on AWS, Azure, and GCP.

json-support.comment: Not a document store; data is modeled as nodes/relationships with typed properties, with no native JSON document type. (APOC offers JSON import/export helpers but not a native queryable JSON type.)

geospatial.comment: Native POINT spatial type (WGS-84 and Cartesian CRS), distance functions, and point indexes.

time-series.comment: No specialized time-series data type or engine; temporal data is modeled in the graph.

stored-procedures.comment: User-defined procedures (Java) callable from Cypher; APOC ships 400+ standard procedures.

triggers.comment: Triggers provided via the official APOC library (disabled by default); not part of the core engine.

views.comment: No view (regular or materialized) concept in the graph model.

official-gui.comment: Official Neo4j Browser and Neo4j Desktop graphical tools.

docker-image.comment: Official 'neo4j' image on Docker Hub maintained by Neo4j.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Approx 8,200 questions tagged [neo4j] (per Neo4j blog, March 2026). Direct Stack Overflow tag page was not fetchable; figure is approximate.

db-engines-rank.comment: Overall DB-Engines rank #20; #1 among graph databases. Checked 2026-06-09.

learning-curve.comment: Graph data model and the Cypher (non-SQL) query language add conceptual overhead, but documentation and tutorials are strong; assessed as moderate (3/5).
